title: ulmus pseudocoritana
intro: Ulmus pseudoploridana, also known as False Elm, is a deciduous tree species native to North America. The bark, leaves, and inner bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Ulmus pseudoploridana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
